circuits.designation: Sauna heater dedicated radial (6 kW single-phase hard-wired BS EN 60335-2-53 appliance) — documented §411 exception: 30 mA RCD impractical due to heater fault-current characteristics, per §703.411.3.3 heater-exclusion language verified in the standards file

cable.csa_mm2_or_awg: 4mm² + 4mm² CPC silicone-insulated (heat-rated for §703 Zone 1 ambient up to 170°C)

circuits.rcd_exception_citation: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §703.411.3.3 (sauna heater circuit excluded from the 30 mA RCD blanket per the verified standards file shared/standards/electrical/BS7671/part7-special-locations.json §703 key requirement: '30 mA RCD on all sauna circuits except the heater (Reg 703.411.3.3)') + §411 top-level (documented-exception path) + BS EN 60335-2-53 (sauna heating appliances — particular requirements). Equipment-side rationale: the heater's low-resistance element exhibits cold-start fault-current characteristics that make a 30 mA RCD impractical without nuisance trips at every commissioning cycle. The BS EN 60335-2-53 appliance type-test certifies the equipment-side safety integrity required for the §411 documented-exception path; the upstream OCPD (32 A Type C MCB) provides §434 overload + short-circuit protection in lieu of the 30 mA additional-protection RCD.

circuits.designation: Sauna control panel dedicated radial — low-voltage timer/thermostat with door-switch interlock, mounted OUTSIDE the sauna cabin on the changing-room face per §703 'all control gear OUTSIDE the sauna' key requirement; standard Type A 30 mA path

non_compliance_flags.message: Two distinct rcd_posture values coexist in the same Part-7 room (sauna). C01 (sauna heater) takes the documented-§411-exception path (no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception) with the rcd_exception_citation populated. C02 (sauna control panel) takes the standard Type A 30 mA path (type_a_30ma_per_§411_3_3). This is the canonical engineering point of the example — the §411 exception is a NARROW carve-out for specific equipment that cannot tolerate 30 mA RCD discrimination, NOT a room-wide exemption. The verified standards file's §703 key requirement language explicitly excludes the heater circuit while requiring 30 mA RCD on all other sauna circuits per §703.411.3.3.

Sauna heater (C01) hard-wired per BS EN 60335-2-53 — no socket inside the sauna cabin. The §703 key requirement that 'only the sauna heater supply may pass through the sauna walls' is respected: C01 enters the cabin via a heat-rated silicone-insulated cable terminating directly at the heater appliance terminals; C02 (control panel) terminates at the control unit mounted on the EXTERNAL face of the changing-room wall.

Sauna cabin (room_type=sauna, Part 7-703 location) contains ZERO 230 V sockets — consistent with §703 key requirements (sockets would breach the high-temperature/IPx4 zone constraints; the heater supply is the only cable permitted through the sauna walls). The control panel mounted on the EXTERNAL face is not a socket.

Cascaded from special-locations: 3-zone derivation (sauna_zone_1 around heater + sauna_zone_2 sauna body + sauna_zone_3 above 1500 mm) from a single sauna_heater anchor per §703 zone-table convention; 1 electrical_constraint of type rcd_blanket_by_room with sauna_heater_excluded=true. The cascade source path points at a DEFERRED-POINTER producer-side fixture (cascade-small-power-uk-sauna-heater-exemption) that D.1 of Phase D will author byte-identical AFTER C.4 ships.

C01 cable specified as silicone-insulated heat-rated 4 mm² + 4 mm² CPC for the §703 Zone 1 ambient (typically 170°C around the heater per the verified standards file's §703 key requirement on cable selection). The schema's insulation enum does not include silicone explicitly — EPR is selected as the closest available enum literal (EPR/EPDM elastomeric rubber families are heat-resistant to ~150°C and represent the schema-pinned approximation of the silicone install). Engineer-of-record substitutes the manufacturer's silicone cable part number at procurement; the IR carries the heat-rated discipline forward.

All Zs verification deferred to calc.zs_loop_impedance per WI3; both circuits have tool_call_pending_for_zs_verification=true and TOOL-CALL-PENDING:calc.zs_loop_impedance is set in flags[]. C01's Zs verification is particularly important — without an upstream 30 mA RCD, the §411 documented-exception path requires the §411.4.5 disconnection-time table to be satisfied entirely by the OCPD characteristic at the prospective fault current. The 32 A Type C MCB on a 6 m run with a declared Ze=0.35 Ω is provisionally within the Zs envelope for §411.4.5 0.4 s domestic-final disconnection, pending tool-side verification.

assumptions: Ze=0.35 Ω and PSCC=6 kA declared by DNO at the consumer cut-out for the UK domestic TN-C-S supply; not yet measured on site. Both values are typical for a UK domestic supply downstream of a 100 A BS 1361 cut-out.

Sauna cabin is a prefabricated 2.4 × 2.0 × 2.4 m timber cabin (typical UK domestic installation in a garden room or basement). The cabin envelope itself is treated as the §703 room polygon; no separate changing room is modelled in this example to keep the focus on the two-posture demonstration. In a full installation IR the changing room would be a sibling room with its own rooms[] entry.

Sauna heater is a 6 kW single-phase hard-wired appliance compliant with BS EN 60335-2-53 (Particular requirements for sauna heating appliances). Design current 6000/230 = 26.1 A continuous. The diversified_max_load_a (26.1 A) equals the design current — no diversity credit applied because the heater is a continuous resistive load that runs at full design current throughout a typical 30-60 minute sauna session.

Sauna control panel (C02) is a low-current draw timer/thermostat unit with a door-switch interlock (the heater is de-energised whenever the cabin door opens). The control panel is mounted on the EXTERNAL face of the changing-room wall per the §703 'all control gear OUTSIDE the sauna' key requirement; the door-switch interlock is the only sensor inside the cabin and runs at extra-low voltage on a separate signal pair (out of scope for this small-power IR — the small-power IR captures the 230 V control panel supply only).

C01 (heater) takes rcd_posture='no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception' with the rcd_exception_citation populated. The exception is the NARROW carve-out for the heater specifically — sibling circuits in the same Part-7 room still default to Type A 30 mA per §411.3.3 (C02 demonstrates the sibling-circuit posture coexisting cleanly in the same room).

C01 OCPD selected as 32 A Type C MCB — Type C curve appropriate for the heater's cold-start inrush characteristic (low-resistance element warming through its thermal coefficient before settling at the steady-state 26.1 A). Type B would risk nuisance tripping during cold-start cycles. Type C's 5-10× tripping band per BS EN 60898-1 accommodates the inrush without compromising §434 short-circuit protection.

C01 cable is specified as silicone-insulated 4 mm² + 4 mm² CPC heat-rated for §703 Zone 1 ambient (170°C per verified standards file). The schema's insulation enum is {PVC_70, XLPE_90, EPR}; silicone is not enumerated. EPR is selected as the closest schema-pinned approximation (heat-resistant elastomeric rubber). Engineer-of-record substitutes the manufacturer's silicone cable part number at procurement — the IR carries the heat-rated discipline forward via the designation text and via this assumption.

C02 cable is standard 1.5 mm² T+E PVC — the cable does NOT enter the sauna cabin (the control panel is on the EXTERNAL face), so the heat-rated requirement does not apply. Standard PVC is appropriate.

rcd_posture for C02 demonstrates the deliberate sibling-circuit treatment: even though C02 is in the same Part-7 sauna room as C01, the §411 exception is NARROW (heater-specific equipment-side fault-current carve-out). C02 takes the standard Type A 30 mA path per §411.3.3 because its low-current control-circuit load is well below the heater's fault-current envelope and does NOT exhibit the heater's nuisance-trip behaviour.

Sauna cabin's room.special_location is set to null (the schema's special_location enum only carries bathroom_zone_*, outdoor, wet_area — no sauna_zone_* literals). The §703 zoning is carried entirely by the cascade payload's zones[] array (3 zones per the verified §703 zone-table convention).

DEFERRED-POINTER cascade-source disclosure: at C.4-ship the consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning.source_path points at a producer-side fixture (electrical/special-locations/examples/cascade-small-power-uk-sauna-heater-exemption/intent-out.json) that does NOT yet exist. D.1 of the Phase D dispatch authors that fixture AFTER C.4 ships. The inlined payload here is what D.1 will emit byte-identical — 3 zones (sauna_zone_1/2/3), 1 electrical_constraint (rcd_blanket_by_room with sauna_heater_excluded=true), compliant=true, zero non-compliance flags. The producer fixture is functionally identical to the existing electrical/special-locations/examples/uk-sauna-with-3-zone-derivation/ fixture but explicitly tagged as a small-power-consumer cascade source.
v2.0 retrofit context: this is a NEW v2.0.0 example — no v1.x retrofit. Both C01 and C02 are dedicated_radial topology so ring_endpoints does not apply to either circuit.

INV-13 (building_diversity self-consistency) N/A and trivially PASS — building_diversity intentionally absent. This is a Part-7 §703 two-posture demonstration, not a building-demand example. INV-14 (ring continuity) N/A — no ring topology circuits. INV-15 (floor-area cross-check) N/A — circuit.floor_area_m2 absent on both circuits. INV-17 (FCU spurs) N/A — no fcu_spurs[] populated. INV-18 (EV RCD Type A/B selection) N/A — no ev_charge_metadata; no circuit matches load_type=ev_charge_*. INV-19 (cable-sizing cascade integration with building_diversity) N/A — both inputs absent.

drawing_notes: Sauna cabin (Part 7-703, room_type=sauna): hard-wired BS EN 60335-2-53 heater fed from C01 dedicated radial via heat-rated silicone-insulated 4 mm² + 4 mm² CPC cable (schema-pinned to EPR as closest enum approximation; engineer-of-record substitutes manufacturer silicone part number at procurement). NO 230 V sockets inside the cabin per §703 key requirements.

C01 sauna heater dedicated radial: 32 A Type C MCB (no RCD — documented §411 exception per §703.411.3.3 heater-exclusion language + BS EN 60335-2-53 appliance type-test). The §411 documented-exception path is a NARROW carve-out for the heater specifically; the rcd_exception_citation field is populated to satisfy validator INV-06 sub-rule 3.

C02 sauna control panel dedicated radial: 6 A RCBO Type B + Type A 30 mA per §411.3.3. Control panel mounted on the EXTERNAL face of the changing-room wall per §703 'all control gear OUTSIDE the sauna' key requirement. The two-posture coexistence (C01 §411-exception + C02 standard §411.3.3) is the canonical engineering point of this example.

Door-switch interlock is wired to C02's low-voltage control circuit (out of scope for this small-power IR — captured at the control panel internal logic). Heater is de-energised whenever the cabin door opens.

All Zs verification deferred to calc.zs_loop_impedance per WI3. C01's Zs verification is particularly critical because the documented-§411-exception path requires §411.4.5 disconnection-time to be satisfied entirely by the OCPD characteristic at the prospective fault current (no upstream 30 mA RCD additional-protection).

BS EN 60335-2-53 (sauna heating appliances — particular requirements) and HD 60364-7-703 (CENELEC harmonisation reference for §703) are cited alongside the BS 7671 install-side clauses; the heater appliance side and the install side together compose the safety case for the §411 documented exception.

rationale.chat_summary: UK domestic sauna room — 2.4 × 2.0 m prefabricated timber cabin with a 6 kW hard-wired BS EN 60335-2-53 heater on a dedicated radial (C01, 32 A Type C MCB, heat-rated silicone-insulated 4 mm² + 4 mm² CPC, no RCD per the documented §411 exception) plus a sauna-control panel on a separate dedicated radial (C02, 6 A RCBO + Type A 30 mA per §411.3.3, low-voltage timer/thermostat with door-switch interlock, mounted OUTSIDE the cabin on the changing-room face). The canonical engineering point: two distinct rcd_posture values coexist in the same Part-7 §703 room — C01 = no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception (heater-specific narrow carve-out per the §703.411.3.3 heater-exclusion language verified in the standards file + BS EN 60335-2-53 appliance side) + C02 = type_a_30ma_per_§411_3_3 (standard sibling-circuit posture). The §411 exception is NOT room-wide; it is equipment-specific. Cascade payload INLINED at C.4-ship (DEFERRED-POINTER to D.1 producer fixture); 3 zones derived per §703 zone-table + 1 rcd_blanket_by_room constraint with sauna_heater_excluded=true. INV-12 PASS (all 4 sub-checks); INV-06 sub-rule 3 PASS (rcd_exception_citation populated on C01); all 19 INVs evaluated.

sections.title: Why room_type=sauna (exact Part-7 enum match)
sections.summary: Sprint D4's binding condition for the Part-7 demonstration examples is that the cascade INV (INV-12) must actually fire. The schema's allOf clause and INV-12's wording both pivot on room_type being one of the Part-7 set: {bathroom, shower_room, swimming_pool_hall, sauna, medical_group_0_area, medical_group_1_ward, medical_group_2_theatre, external_landscape}. This example uses the exact 'sauna' literal so the allOf clause fires and INV-12 enters its 4 sub-check sequence.

decisions.label: room_type literal selection
decisions.summary: Set rooms[0].room_type='sauna' (no _domestic, _commercial, or _room suffix). The Part 7-703 enum trigger then activates the schema's allOf clause requiring consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning to be populated.
decisions.rule: Part-7 enum trigger in small-power IR schema allOf clause

sections.title: Why the heater takes the documented-§411-exception path
sections.summary: The verified standards file (shared/standards/electrical/BS7671/part7-special-locations.json §703 key_requirements) records the heater-exclusion language verbatim: '30 mA RCD on all sauna circuits except the heater (Reg 703.411.3.3)'. The equipment-side rationale is that BS EN 60335-2-53 sauna heating appliances exhibit low-resistance heater elements with cold-start fault-current characteristics that make a 30 mA RCD impractical without nuisance trips at every commissioning cycle. The heater is hard-wired (no socket) and is the only cable permitted to pass through the sauna walls per another verified §703 key requirement. Together these conditions justify rcd_posture='no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception' on C01 with rcd_exception_citation populated.

decisions.label: rcd_posture=no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception on C01
decisions.summary: Set C01.rcd_posture to the schema enum literal for the documented-exception path. Populate rcd_exception_citation with the explicit §703.411.3.3 + §411 top-level + BS EN 60335-2-53 reasoning so validator INV-06 sub-rule 3 PASSes (sub-rule 3 hard-fails if the citation is missing).
decisions.rule: INV-06 sub-rule 3 — documented exception requires non-empty citation

decisions.label: OCPD = 32 A Type C MCB (no RCD)
decisions.summary: Type C curve (5-10× tripping band per BS EN 60898-1) accommodates the heater's cold-start inrush without nuisance trips; Type B would risk nuisance tripping during cold-start cycles. With no upstream 30 mA RCD, the §411.4.5 disconnection-time table must be satisfied entirely by the MCB characteristic at the prospective fault current — deferred to calc.zs_loop_impedance for verification.
decisions.rule: Heater inrush + §411.4.5 disconnection-time satisfied by OCPD characteristic alone

sections.title: Why the control panel keeps the standard §411.3.3 30 mA posture (two postures coexist)
sections.summary: The §411 exception is a NARROW equipment-specific carve-out, NOT a room-wide exemption. The §703 key requirement language explicitly says 'except the heater' — every OTHER sauna circuit still takes the 30 mA RCD blanket per §703.411.3.3 (which references back to §411.3.3 as the install-side default). C02 (sauna control panel) is a low-current draw timer/thermostat with door-switch interlock; its load envelope is well below the heater's fault-current characteristic and does NOT exhibit nuisance-trip behaviour. C02 therefore takes rcd_posture='type_a_30ma_per_§411_3_3' — the standard sibling-circuit posture coexisting cleanly in the same Part-7 room.

decisions.label: rcd_posture=type_a_30ma_per_§411_3_3 on C02
decisions.summary: C02 is a low-current control circuit (estimated 0.05 kW = ~0.2 A typical, sized at 0.5 A diversified for headroom). 30 mA Type A RCD is the standard §411.3.3 posture for all socket and fixed-equipment circuits ≤32 A. The control panel cable (1.5 mm² T+E PVC) does not enter the sauna cabin (control gear mounted OUTSIDE per §703 key requirement), so the heat-rated cable discipline does not apply to C02.
decisions.rule: Standard §411.3.3 30 mA RCD default for socket and fixed-equipment circuits ≤32 A

decisions.label: Two-posture coexistence as the canonical engineering point
decisions.summary: The example is deliberately constructed to demonstrate that the §411 exception path is NARROW — equipment-specific carve-outs do not propagate to sibling circuits. The non_compliance_flags[0] info-severity message captures this engineering point for the runtime to surface in the design review.

sections.title: Why the heater cable is heat-rated (silicone) and schema-pinned to EPR
sections.summary: The verified §703 key_requirements language: 'Cables must be rated for sauna temperatures (typically 170°C in zone 1) — silicone insulated or equivalent'. The C01 cable enters sauna_zone_1 around the heater anchor where the ambient routinely sits at 170°C. Silicone-insulated heat-rated cable is the engineering choice. The small-power IR schema's insulation enum is {PVC_70, XLPE_90, EPR}; silicone is not enumerated. EPR (ethylene propylene rubber, elastomeric, heat-resistant to ~150°C) is the closest schema-pinned approximation. The cable's csa_mm2_or_awg designation text carries the silicone-insulated heat-rated discipline forward; the assumption record documents the engineer-of-record substitution at procurement.

decisions.label: Schema-pinned insulation=EPR (closest approximation to silicone)
decisions.summary: Schema enum {PVC_70, XLPE_90, EPR} does not include silicone. EPR is the closest heat-resistant elastomeric family. The IR carries the silicone discipline in csa_mm2_or_awg text + assumptions[] + drawing_notes; the enum literal is the schema-valid approximation.

sections.title: DEFERRED-POINTER cascade payload (D.1 will populate byte-identical)
sections.summary: consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning.source_path points to electrical/special-locations/examples/cascade-small-power-uk-sauna-heater-exemption/intent-out.json — a producer-side fixture that does NOT yet exist at C.4-ship. D.1 of the Phase D dispatch authors that fixture AFTER C.4 ships. The inlined payload at C.4-ship is what D.1 will emit byte-identical: 3 zones (zone_heater_1_z1 around the sauna_heater anchor, zone_heater_1_z2 covering the sauna body polygon, zone_heater_1_z3 above 1500 mm for accessory mounting) per the verified §703 zone-table convention; 1 electrical_constraint of type rcd_blanket_by_room with sauna_heater_excluded=true reflecting the verified §703.411.3.3 heater-exclusion language; compliant=true, zone_count=3, constraint_count=1, all violation counts 0, non_compliance_flags=[]. The producer fixture is functionally identical to the existing electrical/special-locations/examples/uk-sauna-with-3-zone-derivation/ fixture but explicitly tagged as a small-power-consumer cascade source — the file-tree naming makes the producer-side intent of 'this is the small-power-consumer source' auditable.

decisions.label: Inline-with-deferred-pointer pattern (mirrors C.3)
decisions.summary: Match the C.3 EV pattern: inline the payload at consumer ship, retrofit source_path at producer ship. Unblocks C.4 without forcing serial D.1→C.4 ordering. At D.1-ship the source_path resolves to a real producer file but the payload bytes remain unchanged.

sections.title: INV-12 cascade walkthrough (4 sub-checks)
sections.summary: Sub-check 1 PASS — consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning is present with the inlined payload. Sub-check 2 PASS — payload.compliant=true, zone_count=3, constraint_count=1 (rcd_blanket_by_room with sauna_heater_excluded=true), violation_count_critical=0, violation_count_high=0. Sub-check 3 PASS — the sauna cabin has zero 230 V sockets to walk against the zone polygons (sockets[]=[]); the control panel mounted on the external face of the changing-room wall is NOT inside the sauna room polygon (and would not be a 230 V socket anyway). The heater is hard-wired, not a socket. Vacuous zone-containment satisfaction. Sub-check 4 PASS — payload.non_compliance_flags=[]; nothing to cascade.

decisions.label: Sub-check 3 vacuous treatment (sockets[]=[])
decisions.summary: The sauna cabin is intentionally socket-free per the §703 key requirement that the heater supply is the only cable permitted through the sauna walls. The zone-containment check is vacuous because there are no sockets to walk against the zone polygons; the heater appliance is hard-wired and the control panel sits outside the room polygon.
decisions.rule: Vacuous zone-containment rule for socket-free Part-7 rooms

sections.title: INV-06 sub-rule 3 walkthrough (rcd_exception_citation populated)
sections.summary: INV-06 sub-rule 3 (hard fail) fires when rcd_posture='no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception' and rcd_exception_citation is empty or missing. C01 carries the documented-exception posture; the rcd_exception_citation field is populated with an explicit BS 7671 §703.411.3.3 + §411 + BS EN 60335-2-53 reference (per the validator prompt's INV-06 sub-rule 3 wording 'explicit BS 7671 §411 / IEC 60364-4-41 §411 reference'). INV-06 PASSes HIGH severity (the validator treats sub-rule 3 as hard-fail-with-citation).

decisions.label: rcd_exception_citation content discipline
decisions.summary: The citation carries three components: (1) the install-side §703.411.3.3 heater-exclusion clause verified in the standards file, (2) the §411 top-level documented-exception path, and (3) the equipment-side BS EN 60335-2-53 sauna heating appliance standard. Together these compose the full §411 documented-exception safety case.

sections.title: INV-13..INV-19 evaluation
sections.summary: INV-13 (building_diversity self-consistency) N/A and trivially PASS — building_diversity intentionally absent. This is a §703 two-posture demonstration, not a building-demand example. INV-14 (ring continuity) N/A and trivially PASS — both circuits are dedicated_radial topology; the ring-endpoints continuity rule does not apply. INV-15 (floor-area cross-check) N/A — circuit.floor_area_m2 not populated. INV-16 (OCPD-topology coordination) PASS — both circuits are dedicated_radial; validator INV-16 wording 'topology=dedicated_radial → MCB sized by connected load per §433.1.1, validator trivially PASSES on dedicated_radial' applies. INV-17 (FCU spurs) N/A — no fcu_spurs[] populated. INV-18 (EV RCD Type A/B selection) N/A and trivially PASS — no ev_charge_metadata; no circuit's load_type matches ev_charge_*. INV-19 (cable-sizing cascade integration with building_diversity) N/A — both inputs absent.

decisions.label: Intentional N/A for INV-13/14/15/17/18/19
decisions.summary: This example's engineering scope is §703 two-posture demonstration via rcd_posture enum on dedicated_radial circuits. building_diversity, ring continuity, floor_area_m2 cross-check, FCU spurs, EV charge metadata, and cable-sizing intent integration are all out of scope; they belong to other Phase A/B/C examples. The N/A-trivially-PASS treatment follows the validator prompt's explicit N/A wording for each.

sections.title: Honest disclosures (verified-citation discipline + deferred-pointer cascade + schema-pinned insulation)
sections.summary: (1) Verified-citation discipline: only §703 top-level + §703.411.3.3 (verified in standards file as the heater-exclusion clause; the single permitted §703 sub-clause for this example, mirroring the C.3 §722.531.3.101 precedent) + §411 + §411.3.3 + §411.4.5 + BS EN 60335-2-53 + HD 60364-7-703 are cited. Banned and do NOT cite: §703.55 / §703.512 / §703.413 / §526.2 / §433.2 / OZEV / 3rd Edition / Reg 559 (inherited 14-sub-clause ban). (2) DEFERRED-POINTER cascade: source_path points to a producer-side fixture that does NOT yet exist at C.4-ship — D.1 of Phase D authors it AFTER C.4 ships. The inlined payload is what D.1 will emit byte-identical. (3) Schema-pinned insulation=EPR is the closest enum approximation to the engineering-correct silicone-insulated heat-rated cable; the IR carries the silicone discipline in csa_mm2_or_awg text + assumptions + drawing_notes. (4) Two-posture coexistence is the canonical engineering point — the §411 exception is NARROW (heater-specific), NOT room-wide; sibling circuits in the same Part-7 room still take the standard §411.3.3 path.

invariants.evidence: Both C01 and C02 are dedicated_radial topology — INV-01 ring composition rule (Table 7.1 / Appendix 15 ring final circuit recipe) does not apply to dedicated_radial circuits. Vacuous PASS on both.

Every circuit's ocpd.breaking_capacity_ka (C01: 6.0; C02: 6.0) is greater than or equal to parent_db.pfc_at_busbar_ka (6.0) per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §434.5.1 and IEC 60898-1 conditional short-circuit ratings. Equality at 6 kA is the §434.5.1 minimum boundary — typical for UK domestic CUs with declared PFC ≤6 kA.

C02 (sauna control panel) carries Type A 30 mA RCD per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §411.3.3 (rcd_posture=type_a_30ma_per_§411_3_3). C01 (sauna heater) takes the documented-§411-exception path (rcd_posture=no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception) with the rcd_exception_citation populated. INV-03 is satisfied because C01's exception is documented per the validator prompt's INV-06 sub-rule 3 path, and C02 follows the standard §411.3.3 default. The two postures coexist cleanly in the same Part-7 room.

Sauna cabin (room_type=sauna, Part 7-703 location) carries ZERO 230 V sockets (sockets[]=[]). The hard-wired BS EN 60335-2-53 heater is the only fixture inside the cabin — fed by C01 entering via heat-rated cable per §703 key requirement 'only the sauna heater supply may pass through the sauna walls'. The control panel for C02 is mounted on the EXTERNAL face of the changing-room wall (out of the sauna room polygon) per §703 key requirement 'all control gear OUTSIDE the sauna'. No socket-in-zone contradiction possible.

C01 (dedicated_radial) rooms_covered=[sauna_cabin] feeds the hard-wired sauna heater. C02 (dedicated_radial) rooms_covered=[sauna_cabin] feeds the control panel mounted on the external face. The cabin's sockets[]=[] so no socket→circuit cross-references to verify; the room appears in both circuits' rooms_covered[] arrays. No orphan rooms; no circuits coverage of a non-existent room.

INV-06 sub-rule 3 is the focus invariant: rcd_posture='no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception' on C01 is accompanied by a non-empty rcd_exception_citation referencing BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §703.411.3.3 (verified heater-exclusion language) + §411 top-level (documented-exception path) + BS EN 60335-2-53 (sauna heating appliances). C02 uses rcd_posture=type_a_30ma_per_§411_3_3 — sub-rule 1 default, no further justification required. Both circuits PASS. Sub-rule 2 (Type B) does not apply — neither circuit uses Type B.

diversified_max_load_a per circuit (C01: 26.1 A; C02: 0.5 A) is strictly less than ocpd.rating_a (C01: 32 A; C02: 6 A). C01's 26.1 A is the heater design current (6000 W / 230 V = 26.087 A rounded to 26.1) with no diversity credit — heater is a continuous resistive load that runs at full design current throughout a sauna session. The 32 A OCPD provides 5.9 A headroom (~22%) against the 32 A breaker rating, well within the §433.1.1 In ≥ Ib coordination.

Both circuits have tool_call_pending_for_zs_verification=true (deferred per WI3). flags[] carries TOOL-CALL-PENDING:calc.zs_loop_impedance. C01's Zs verification is particularly important — without an upstream 30 mA RCD additional-protection, the §411.4.5 disconnection-time table must be satisfied entirely by the 32 A Type C MCB characteristic at the prospective fault current. The 6 m run + Ze=0.35 Ω + 4 mm² CPC is provisionally within the 0.4 s domestic-final disconnection envelope, pending tool-side verification at calc.zs_loop_impedance.

The single room (sauna_cabin) appears in both C01 and C02 rooms_covered[] arrays. Both circuits serve the same room (heater + control panel for the same cabin). sockets[]=[] so there are no orphan sockets; the room is not orphan because both circuits cover it.

drawing_layout.drawing_standard=BS 1192:2007+A2:2016 matches the GB jurisdiction expected default for small-power layout drawings. Sheet size A3 + scale 1:50 is the engineer-justified override for a small single-room sauna layout (the inputs.json default is A1; A3 is appropriate for a 2.4 × 2.0 m room footprint and is documented in compliance_summary.assumptions). INV-10 emits a low-severity warning permitted by the engineer override discipline.

No cable-sizing intent consumed in this example (consumed_intents.cable_sizing absent). INV-11 is a no-op per the v1.1 conditional rule; both circuits carry tool_call_pending_for_zs_verification=true to defer Zs to calc.zs_loop_impedance. When a cable-sizing intent is later consumed, INV-11 will reconcile design_current_a against diversified_max_load_a (expected match at 26.1 A for C01 heater and at 0.5 A for C02 control panel).

Cascade source (DEFERRED-POINTER): electrical/special-locations/examples/cascade-small-power-uk-sauna-heater-exemption/intent-out.json (D.1 producer-side fixture pending; payload INLINED byte-identical at C.4-ship). Sub-check 1 PASS (consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning present). Sub-check 2 PASS (payload.compliant=true, zone_count=3 sauna_zone_1/2/3 per §703 zone-table, constraint_count=1 rcd_blanket_by_room with sauna_heater_excluded=true, zero violations). Sub-check 3 PASS — sockets[]=[] in the sauna cabin so zone-containment is vacuous; the hard-wired heater is not a socket and the control panel sits outside the room polygon. Sub-check 4 PASS — payload.non_compliance_flags=[]; nothing to cascade. BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §703 + §703.411.3.3 + §411 + BS EN 60335-2-53.

N/A and trivially PASS per validator INV-13 wording — building_diversity block intentionally absent. This is a Part-7 §703 two-posture demonstration example, not a building-demand example; building_diversity belongs to Phase A/B examples that exercise the office/healthcare/industrial demand cascade.

N/A and trivially PASS — neither circuit is topology=ring. C01 and C02 are both dedicated_radial; ring continuity (TOP-09 / IET OSG §8.4.4) does not apply to dedicated_radial circuits. Validator INV-14 evaluates ring continuity only on ring topology circuits.

N/A and trivially PASS per validator INV-15 wording — circuit.floor_area_m2 is not populated on either circuit (v2.0 optional field). The rule fires only when both floor_area_m2 AND rooms_covered[] are populated and demands Σ-tolerance reconciliation; with floor_area_m2 absent, no reconciliation is computed.

C01 (dedicated_radial, 32 A Type C MCB on 4 mm² + 4 mm² CPC schema-pinned-EPR-as-silicone-approximation, sized to the 26.1 A heater design current with §433.1.1 In ≥ Ib coordination). C02 (dedicated_radial, 6 A RCBO Type B on 1.5 mm² T+E PVC, sized to the 0.5 A control panel diversified load). Validator INV-16 wording 'topology=dedicated_radial → MCB sized by connected load per §433.1.1, validator trivially PASSES on dedicated_radial' applies to both circuits.

N/A and trivially PASS per validator INV-17 wording — no circuit has fcu_spurs[] populated. Rule TOP-12 evaluates only when fcu_spurs[] is populated. FCU spurs are appropriate only on ring final circuits per IET OSG §8.4.4; both circuits in this example are dedicated_radial so FCU spurs do not apply.

N/A and trivially PASS per validator INV-18 wording — no ev_charge_metadata block populated on either circuit; no circuit's load_type matches ev_charge_*. Rule EV-03 evaluates only on EV charge circuits. The sauna installation does not include any EV charge equipment.

N/A and trivially PASS per validator INV-19 wording — both building_diversity.per_circuit_demand_inputs[] AND consumed_intents.cable_sizing.payload.circuits[] are absent. Rule BLD-05 evaluates only when both are present and reconciles post_per_load_diversity_a with cable-sizing's design_current_a within ±5% tolerance.

zones._derivation_note: Zone 1 = heater footprint padded 500mm per §703 zone-table; heat-rated cable; heater circuit excluded from RCD blanket per §703.411.3.3.
Zone 2 = sauna body polygon (full room minus Zone 1); runtime subtracts Zone 1 for plan rendering.
Zone 3 = full sauna polygon at height_min=1500mm to ceiling; upper-volume zone for accessories.

electrical_constraints._derivation_note: 30 mA RCD on all sauna LV circuits EXCEPT sauna heater per §703.411.3.3.
